methodology: WMA working-band ensemble (fixed-EW, u10, fillna(0)). Select on TRAIN+VAL within-2020 (6/3/3 split). OOS=Oct-Dec 2020 (ironed sleeve: trail0.10+minhold12+lag1+maker 0.0006RT). All-weather via rolling-from-band 2020-2022.

per_tf.notes: High entry-lag (0.83): band waits for daily trend confirmation, misses most sub-daily moves. Low 5%-move coverage (0.29). Worst 2022 bear among coarse TFs (-35.6%). Only 0.9 RT/yr -- suitable for low-frequency operators.

Largest band (114 members): maximum ensemble diversification. Best 2022 bear preservation (-30.8%) among all TFs. Strong OOS (+21.9%). p05 -4.1%. Coverage jumps from 1d to 4h (+22pp). Sweet spot for weekly rebalancers.

BEST TF: highest OOS net (+29.4%) and best bootstrap p05 (-3.2%) of all 6 TFs. Tighter entry lag vs 4h (0.64 vs 0.70). Coverage 0.595 (further +9pp vs 4h). 2022 bear -33.4% -- slightly worse than 4h but OOS/p05 advantage dominates.

Best p05 of all TFs (-1.7%), lowest maxDD (-8.3%), highest coverage (0.626) and weighted capture (0.339). Strong OOS (+26.9%). At 1h the band is fully in the move before peak -- entry lag 0.584 means average entry near the first half of moves. 13 RT/yr/asset manageable.

Performance cliff begins: OOS falls to +19.4% (vs +26.9% at 1h) and 2022 bear worsens to -41.8%. Band shrinks to 79 members (-30 vs 1h). 2021 bull halves vs swing TFs. Cost pressure at 20.5 RT/yr. Entry lag stagnates (0.595 -- no improvement vs 1h).

Worst TF: 2022 bear deepest (-47.6%), 2021 bull lowest (142%), OOS lowest (+17.9%), p05 worst (-6.9%), band smallest (46). Entry lag INCREASES vs 1h (0.604 > 0.584) -- selectivity degrades at 15m. 29.5 RT/yr/asset with maker cost bites significantly.

per_asset_note: Per-asset band-selection vs pooled-within-type: NOISE. The PER_ASSET.md analysis (2020 OOS) shows per-asset sleeve-family tailoring sits at the 1.5th percentile of random assignment on 1d and 34.5th on 4h -- both below the uniform trend band. Cross-asset breadth (10-asset EW book, pairwise corr ~0.35 at 4h, n_eff ~2.4) is the real diversifier; per-asset config-rank rho is ~0 OOS (D62, dead-list). Conclusion: use the pooled u10 EW band ensemble; do NOT select configs per-asset.
intraday_suitability: Poor. At 30m and 15m the WMA band deteriorates on all three axes: (1) 2022 bear collapses to -41.8% and -47.6% vs -30.8% at 4h; (2) band membership shrinks 79/46 (vs 113-114 at swing), reducing ensemble diversification; (3) 2021 bull return roughly halves vs 4h-2h. Turnover rises to 20-30 RT/yr/asset, increasing cost friction. Entry lag does NOT improve below 1h (0.584->0.595->0.604). WMA is suboptimal at intraday TFs -- use only if capital constraints mandate sub-1h execution.
longterm_suitability: Moderate. The 1d band delivers solid OOS (+19%) with a positive 3-way split, but 5%-move coverage is only 29% (vs 50-63% at swing TFs) and entry lag is highest (0.83). The 2022 bear at -35.6% is worse than 4h (-30.8%). The 1d sleeve is suitable for daily-rebalanced operators or liquidity-constrained deployments (only 0.9 RT/yr/asset). However, the 2h-1h window dominates on OOS net, bear preservation, coverage, and p05 -- if execution allows, always prefer swing TFs.
verdict: WMA is a SWING-TF MA type: it peaks at 2h (OOS +29.4%, p05 -3.2%, 2022 bear -33.4%) and 1h (OOS +26.9%, p05 -1.7%, 2022 bear -33.9%, best coverage 0.626, lowest maxDD -8.3%), delivering clean trend-following with moderate entry lag (0.58-0.64) and manageable turnover (7-13 RT/yr). Coarsening to 1d sacrifices coverage and worsens the bear. Fining to 30m/15m collapses bear preservation (-42/-48%) and erodes the band. Deploy WMA at 2h as the optimal single TF, or run a 1h+2h twin-sleeve for the tightest p05 floor.
